page: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core CPU stress tests, Passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth benchmarks, with cached read-only speeds reaching 4503.04 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 98.33 GB/sec. It also demonstrates high throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in single-threaded stress-ng and gzip compression tests. Large-model text generation benchmarks also show poor performance, though prompt processing speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5.24xlarge offers high memory density and dedicated compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The combination of 768 GB of DDR4 RAM and 96 vCPUs makes this instance highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput Redis deployments, caching layers, and enterprise-grade database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, with cached read-only speeds of 4503.04 GB/sec and strong Redis SET throughput. While single-core performance and text-generation speeds are weaker, its massive memory capacity and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth make it highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring ninety-six virtual CPUs and seven hundred sixty-eight gigabytes of RAM for demanding in-memory workloads.

768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and caching layers
